column11: 1215: Got Underway Noon: Off Lisbon, Portugal.

1630: cc S1/2E. 1600: Farol do Cabo Espichel brg NxW dist 6 mi.
0030: cc SExE3/4E. 0045 Cabo de Sao Vicente Lt brg N, dist 12 mi. 2400: Rounding Cape St. Vincent.
0600: Some Light brg NExN dist 5 mi. Probably Farol da Ponta da Piedade.

0810: cc NE. 0815 Farol da Berlenga brg WxN1/4N dist 2 mi.

1630: Took departure on a line between Farol da Berlenga and Farol do Cabo Carvoeiro abt 11/2 mi from the latter.
1730: Ons Id LH b rg ENE and Cabo Corrubedo NNE.
0000: Faro de Finisterra brg SExE dist 10 mi.
Noon: Noon Lon hard to read degrees. 1230: cc ESE.
1815: Faro do Cabo Prior brg SExS1/2S dist 5 mi. 1830 cc NE. 1845 cc NExE.
1145: Sighted Tower of Hercules, cc SExS.
1840: Tower of Hercules brg SxW1/2W psc dist 31/2 mi.
1530: Got Underway. Stood up the harbor.
column23: 1530: Note: At 3:30 hove up anchor, ship riding to the flood tide. The Niagara steamed toward the entrance of the harbor, apparently with the intention of getting more room to turn round in. When our anchor was away, we backed the engine, moving all the time nearer the city, to give the Niagara more room to turn in. When the Niagara bore off our st'bd bow, and she was still going ahead (our engine backing), Belem Castle first opened fire, one shot struck the water astern of the Niagara. We still continued to back, casting to port until half-way round, when we went ahead and turned the ship's head toward the city and waited for the Niagara to pass us. Belem Castle fired several shots at the Niagara, even when she was more than half-way round and her flag at half mast. The Niagara was some distance inside the Castle when it opened fire. The Sacramento was not fired at.

column11: 1300: Cape Roca Lighthouse brg SExE1/2E psc.
1700: Farol da Berlenga brg W T dist 11/2 mi.
2145: Sisarga Grande Lighthouse brg SE1/2E psc dist 8 mi.

1000: Ushant Id Lights brg E psc dist 6 mi. At 1010 Lights were in line brg ExS psc. 1040 cc NExE1/2E.
Noon: LH on E end UShant Id brg S, LH on W end b rg SSE psc.
0830: Start Point Lighthouse brg NNW 5 mi.
1450: Got Underway passing nearly 4 mi outside the South Foreland Lts.
The pressure here takes a rather large jump. It looks suspicious. Maybe the baro at 0000 should be 29.64.
0615: Got Underway. Slowly, lack of steam. cc Ed.
Noon: Changed Bearo 29.84 to 29.64. A jump of 0.28! in 4 hours seems excessive, especially with a fall of 0.09 four hours later. Noon: Flushing, Netherlands.
I changed Baro 30.26 to 29.96. 30.26 was obviously wrong.
0600: Got Underway. Stood down the channel.
1300: Beachy Head LH brg NE1/2E psc dist 4 mi.
2000: Anchored. Southampton, England. No time given 4-8 PM.
1720: Got Underway. Stood thru the Needles.
2030: The Needles LH brg E1/2N dist 7 mi.
Noon: Lizards brg N1/2W psc dist 8 mi.

1830: Tuskar Lt brg SE1/2E dist 1/2 mi. (Best guess on name)
0000: Passed the shoals above the red light.
I changed Baro 29.64 to 29.84. I think the log writer misread an 8 as a 6.
2317: Blackwater Lt brg NW1/4W psc dist 3 mi.
1530: Got Underway, but lay in the stream.

Noon: Off W end Ilha de Sao Jorge.

column23: 0300: Aurora Borealis burning brightly from 3 to 4.

column11: N: Noon Long might be 56. The DR is 56 5W.
